title: SessionStart wiring: session registration, identity-threaded preamble, handoff-kind branch, two-session fixture
action: Wire the fresh-session flow into SessionStart (per D1; the S2-deferred W-4 preamble threading). PURITY PIN (panel W2): buildSessionPreamble stays a PURE builder — the mutation (adoption) happens in hooks/bee-session-init.mjs, which passes the resolved outcome into the builder. hooks/bee-session-init.mjs: (1) register/refresh the acting session record from payload.session_id (createSession-or-heartbeat via the repo's vendored lib claims.mjs, fail-open — a registration failure never blocks the preamble); (2) EVENT-SCOPE PIN (panel W1): the planned-next adopt+start-now path runs ONLY when payload.source is 'clear' or 'startup' (the fresh-session boundaries D1 names); on 'resume' and 'compact' the hook NEVER adopts — a planned-next handoff renders as a pending-wait block (it stays on disk for the fresh session); additionally refuse start-now when the handoff's writer_session equals the acting session on a non-clear source; (3) when the source qualifies, call fsh-9's adoptHandoff(sessionId) in the hook and pass the outcome into buildSessionPreamble. inject.mjs buildSessionPreamble (pure, takes the outcome as a param): PLANNED-NEXT adopted → replace the wait-block with a start-now block naming the adopted cell, its verify command, and its lane — the fresh session begins that task without asking (D1); adoption failed/skipped (already adopted, steal lost, wrong source, refusal) → the pause presentation with the reason shown. PAUSE (and every kindless legacy handoff) → today's byte-identical present-and-WAIT block. No handoff → today's preamble (with the bound-lane view when a binding exists). Hook-contract rows: (a) planned-next payload with source 'clear' through the REAL hook child → stdout carries the start-now block and the claim's owner flipped to the new session; (b) the SAME payload with source 'resume'/'compact' → pending-wait block, handoff intact, claim owner unchanged; (c) pause and kindless handoffs → byte-identical wait block; (d) no session_id → byte-identical to today; (e) every pre-existing row passes unmodified. Two-session end-to-end fixture (test_lib.mjs, direct lib): session A caps its cell, writes the planned-next handoff carrying the claim, session B adopts (the /clear boundary), a third session's simultaneous steal attempt loses with the typed failure — the epic-map E4 proof riding fsh-2's race harness patterns where real concurrency is needed. RED-first. Vendor byte-identical siblings (cmp chain in verify).

truths: Through the real hook child with source 'clear', a planned-next handoff yields a start-now preamble naming the adopted cell, and the claim's ownership actually transfers to the payload's session
The same planned-next payload on source 'resume' or 'compact' never adopts: the handoff stays on disk, the claim owner is unchanged, and the preamble shows a pending-wait block
Pause and kindless handoffs render today's wait block byte-identically; payloads without session_id render today's preamble byte-identically; all pre-existing rows pass unmodified
The two-session fixture proves cap → handoff → adopt across the boundary with a concurrent third-session steal losing typed
buildSessionPreamble performs no mutation — adoption happens in the hook and the builder renders the passed outcome; a failed adoption never fabricates a start-now instruction

artifacts.substantive: pure handoff-kind rendering: start-now for an adopted planned-next outcome, byte-identical wait for pause/legacy, typed-failure fallback

key_links: bee-session-init threads payload.session_id + payload.source, performs the source-gated adoption via fsh-9's adoptHandoff, and passes the outcome into the pure builder
prohibitions: No changes to guards, write-guard, chain-nudge, session-close
No weakened or deleted existing assertion

verify: node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs && node hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs && cmp -s hooks/bee-session-init.mjs .bee/bin/hooks/bee-session-init.mjs && cmp -s skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/inject.mjs .bee/bin/lib/inject.mjs

trace.outcome: SessionStart wiring for the fresh-session-handoff flow (D1): hooks/bee-session-init.mjs registers/refreshes the acting session (fail-open), then source-gates adoption to payload.source 'clear'/'startup' only ('resume'/'compact' and a same-session 'startup' never adopt), calling fsh-9's adoptHandoff and passing the typed outcome into inject.mjs's now-pure buildSessionPreamble(root, {sessionId, handoffOutcome}). The builder renders a start-now block (adopted cell + lane + verify) on ok:true, a wait block + one reason line on ok:false, and stays byte-identical to before this cell for pause/kindless handoffs and any no-session_id payload. Proven through the real hook child (9 new hook-contract rows including the mandatory resume/compact negative rows) plus a two-session end-to-end race fixture (session A caps+claims+hands off, session B adopts across the simulated /clear boundary, concurrent thieves lose typed CLAIMED) riding fsh-2's fork/barrier-file pattern via a generated temp orchestrator, never touching race_claims_child.mjs.
